core_loop: Practitioner (TS≥30+Approach Training) Leaps (Spirit×2+History+TPS, TN7) to suspend rendering, then performs one op (Weave/Pull/POP/Lock/Dissolve/Mend) at a Depth×Breadth×Distance Ob, paying personal Coherence (10→0) plus a world-track cost (named MS in threadwork_v30, RS in params/threadwork.md — same track). The world track's band × node-distance-from-Askeheim sets ambient Gap/Shifting-Object/Ob pressure for everyone in-territory, practitioner or not.

decision_points.options: build (brittle, Over-Actualisation) vs open (transient, snap-back Wound)
Lock (chronic freeze) vs Dissolution (acute tear, Gap always-on)
conceal (Cognition roll) vs risk Church Attention/CV drift
gamble recovery (season withdrawal+3 Anchoring Scenes+roll, perma -1 TS) vs drift to NPC
pace safe 0-Coherence Mending across season vs let Gaps accrue
spend Knot benefits (Thread-Read, buffer, counsel) vs accrue rupture strain
Anchor+Helper dice-pool coalition vs contested-intentionality resolution

north_star.collision_feed: Thread ops write Domain Echo to faction stats, CV drift, settlement Order, and NPC Conviction Scars (ED-673/675/676) — a genuine cross-scale collision generator. But two of the narrative sinks meant to render these collisions (combat_engine_v1, articulation) are structurally absent, so collisions compute but often can't surface.
north_star.verdict: Points strongly at the North Star mechanically (rare non-dominant, multi-axis decision space) but is undercut by legibility debt (MS/RS split) and missing narrative sinks that prevent its rich collisions from actually reaching the player.

nq.observation: Non-dominance protected by design: Structural/Foundational Mending intentionally ~22-58% even at max pool, preventing a solo-practitioner 'solve the Calamity' strategy.
threadwork_v30 §2.4 retains full STRUCK pre-PP-622 single-axis Ob tables inline for Weave/Pull/Lock/Dissolve, doubling live-vs-history parse cost against the 'restatable after one reading' bar.
Same world track carries two different canonical names (MS vs RS) across documents both treated as live canonical — a direct composability/naming failure.

threadwork_junctures.evidence: threadwork_v30.md cites only superseded combat_v30 §10, never combat_engine_v1; ED-911 open P1
zero Thread/Gap/Rendering-Crisis hooks despite ED-681 authoring 4 narrative beats explicitly for videogame implementation
thread_horizontal_integration_spec.md ED-679: 'Church does not use Thread operations... Thread-resistant by design'

legibility.note: Whose-position/what-actors-want/what-happens-if-no-action are all answerable from calamity_radiation_v30's radiation matrix and threadwork's Coherence table — but a reader cannot state in one sentence which world track is actually failing without first resolving the live MS-vs-RS naming split across two documents both called canonical.

degenerate_candidates: threadwork_v30.md §2.4 ST-TW-01: Object-scale Weaving in a protected combat round costs 0 Coherence and 0 world-track change on Success — self-flagged 'is this intended? See ED-046', and ED-046 does not exist in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl (orphan, never adjudicated).

edges.mechanism: Thread event -> faction stat +-1 via Domain Echo (ED-673)
territory-level radiation trigger -> settlement Order effect (ED-675)
Coherence recovery, Knot strain from opposing ops, Rendering Crisis Anchoring beats
none — only superseded combat_v30 §10 has Leap-in-combat
none — Rendering Crisis beats (ED-681) have no consuming Key/trigger

directions.top-down: P-14 inseparability is honored broadly (faction/settlement/social/fieldwork) but fails at its two highest narrative-value sinks: combat and articulation.
directions.bottom-up: params/threadwork.md's Three-Axis Ob correctly supersedes threadwork_v30's retained pre-PP-622 tables, but the retention itself is a live parse cost, not just patch history.
directions.lateral: Knots and Threadwork compose cleanly and knots_v30 resolved its own internal contradictions (§11) — an instructive contrast to threadwork's still-unresolved MS/RS naming question.
directions.diagonal: Thread's cross-scale wiring into faction/settlement/mass-battle is the richest collision feed in the corpus, but rarely reaches player-visible payoff since articulation doesn't consume it.
directions.forwards: A Godot typed-engine-params export (CLAUDE.md §5) cannot bind a Resource field to this track until MS vs RS is resolved to one name.
directions.backwards: The MS/RS naming question has been relitigated at least three times in the ledger (ED-428 keep RS, ED-731 propagate MS, ED-772 continue MS sweep) and the sweep never reached params/threadwork.md, which still reads RS.

findings.claim: The world risk track is named MS (Mending Stability) in threadwork_v30/calamity_radiation/knots_v30 but RS (Rendering Stability) in the newer canonical params/threadwork.md — same track, both docs treated as live canonical, and the 2026-04-20/04-30 MS-propagation sweep (ED-731/772) never reached params/threadwork.md.
findings.cite: params/threadwork.md L136-138; threadwork_v30.md §5 MODE APPLICABILITY INDEX; canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl ED-428/731/772
findings.tracked_ref: ED-428, ED-731, ED-772 (naming flip-flop history); adds new evidence that the residual divergence is still live post-sweep

findings.claim: combat_engine_v1, the ratified combat head, has zero threadwork surface — confirmed by direct read: threadwork_v30.md cites only the superseded combat_v30 §10 layer.
findings.tracked_ref: ED-911 (open, P1) — restated with direct textual confirmation only, not novel

findings.claim: articulation_layer_v30, the sole narrative-surfacing sink, has zero Thread/Gap/Rendering-Crisis hooks despite ED-681 authoring 4 dedicated narrative beats explicitly 'for videogame implementation' — the richest scripted Thread narrative content has no rendering path.
findings.cite: threadwork_v30.md Rendering Crisis beats (ED-681 embedded text); grounding 03 §3
findings.tracked_ref: grounding 03 §3 lists this absence; no ED exists — adding severity assessment since ED-681's content is explicitly VG-targeted

findings.claim: threadwork_v30.md's own ST-TW-01 design note flags a candidate degenerate line (free Object-scale Weaving in protected combat rounds) and cites 'ED-046' for adjudication, but ED-046 does not exist anywhere in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl — never registered, never resolved.

ED-679 (Thread Warfare NPC AI doctrine) resurrection cost is higher than 'never propagated': its Niflhel §8.8 target section is independently STRUCK from npc_behavior_v30, so simple patch re-application is no longer possible — it needs re-authoring against the current faction roster.
findings.tracked_ref: grounding 03 §4 flags ED-679 never-propagated; adds new cost evidence (target faction gone)

findings.claim: threadwork_v30.md §2.4 retains the full STRUCK pre-PP-622 single-axis Ob tables inline for Weave/Pull/Lock/Dissolve alongside canonical-pointer footnotes, doubling the live-vs-history parse burden against the Q-elegant 'restatable after one reading' bar.
findings.cite: threadwork_v30.md §2.4 (four Ob-by-scale tables, each footnoted pre-PP-622)

subsystem: threadwork (Coherence/MS-RS/Calamity risk economy, Knots, calamity radiation, horizontal integration spec)
